tagline: The jungle war road and one of Asia's most biodiverse forests
description: Changlang is the easternmost tourist district of Arunachal Pradesh. It has two draws that are completely different from each other: Namdapha National Park — one of the most biodiverse protected areas in Asia — and the Stilwell Road, the 1,736km WWII supply route built through this jungle in 1942–44.
incitetales_angle: Namdapha is the most biodiverse national park in India that nobody talks about. Four big cat species in one park.

destinations.name: Namdapha National Park
destinations.tagline: Four big cats, one jungle, almost no tourists
destinations.description: Namdapha is India's third-largest national park and one of its most extraordinary — 1,985 sq km of forest ranging from tropical to alpine, home to tiger, leopard, snow leopard, and clouded leopard (the only park in the world with all four), Hoolock gibbon, red panda, and over 1,000 plant species. The base is Miao town.
what_is_there: Four big cat species — tiger, leopard, snow leopard, clouded leopard
Hoolock gibbons — hear them at dawn from Deban rest house
Namdapha River — crystal clear, runs through the park
Jungle walks with forest guides — Deban to Hornbill Camp trail

stays.name: Deban Forest Rest House
stays.notes: Inside the park, on the Namdapha River bank. Best base. Book via Namdapha Field Director, Miao. Book months ahead.

stays.name: Hornbill Camp
stays.notes: Deeper inside, basic. For serious trekkers.

local_food: Cook at Deban prepares basic meals — arrange in advance
local_tips: Book Deban rest house at least 2 months ahead — it has very few rooms
Gibbons are most vocal at dawn — sit outside Deban by 5:30am
Hire a forest guide through the park office — mandatory and worth every rupee
Namdapha River is safe to swim in near Deban — clear, cold, beautiful
The road inside the park is rough — 4WD essential

destinations.name: Miao
destinations.tagline: The base town for Namdapha and Stilwell Road
destinations.description: Miao is a small town on the Noa-Dihing River, the base for Namdapha. It has basic hotels, the park permit office, and is the last town with any services before the park. The Stilwell Road starts properly here.
local_food: Local dhabas — basic Assamese and Arunachali food
local_tips: Get Namdapha permits here before entering park

destinations.name: Stilwell Road
destinations.tagline: 1,736km of jungle, monsoon, and will. Most of it swallowed back by forest.
destinations.description: The Stilwell Road (also called Ledo Road) was built by 40,000 Allied troops in 1942–44 to supply Nationalist China after Japan cut the Burma Road. It runs from Ledo in Assam through Changlang district of Arunachal, into Myanmar, and on to Yunnan, China. The Indian portion is driveable in sections. WWII-era bridges, mile markers, and abandoned equipment still exist in the jungle.
what_is_there: Mile 0 marker at Ledo (Assam) — small monument at the official start
WWII-era stone bridges — still standing in the jungle sections
Mile markers — concrete posts with mileage, some still legible
Pangsau Pass — India-Myanmar border crossing on the Stilwell Road (restricted, special permit needed)
Nampong town — last Indian town on the road before Myanmar
Dense jungle — the road disappears into forest in sections, exactly as it looked in 1945
local_food: Tangsa and Wancho tribal food if invited
local_tips: Pangsau Pass crossing requires special permit — not available for regular tourists
4WD essential for the jungle sections of the road
Hire a local jeep driver from Miao — they know which sections are driveable
The Digboi WWII cemetery in Assam (nearby) gives essential context before driving the road
Best Oct–Feb — monsoon makes the jungle sections impassable

permit.how_to_apply: Online at arunachalilp.com or Arunachal House
permit.pangsau_pass_notes: Pangsau Pass crossing requires additional restricted area permit — not routinely issued to tourists. Check with Ministry of Home Affairs.

day_splits.notes: Or take overnight train Guwahati → Dibrugarh/Tinsukia. Much easier.
Leave Tinsukia by 8am. Reach Miao by early afternoon. Register at Namdapha office same day.

connectivity.miao: Airtel and BSNL in Miao town.
connectivity.atm: No ATM in Miao. Carry cash from Dibrugarh or Tinsukia.

best_season.notes: Dry season only. Monsoon makes the jungle road and park trails impassable. Winter is excellent — cold nights but clear days.
